00001| TASK: Improve daemon skill inference with description-aware heuristics and accuracy tracking
00002| 
00003| Two gaps in daemon skill inference (pkg/daemon/skill_inference.go): (1) DESCRIPTION-AWARE HEURISTICS: Current inference only uses issue type + skill:* labels + title prefix. It never reads the description. Add keyword/pattern matching on description text to improve inference. Signal words: 'audit/analyze/correlate/investigate/understand/how does' → investigation, 'fix/broken/error/crash/fails' → systematic-debugging (when cause is described) or architect (when vague), 'compare/evaluate/research/best practice' → research. 00051| ### Models (synthesized understanding)
00052| - Daemon Autonomous Operation
00053|   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/daemon-autonomous-operation.md
00054|   - Summary:
00055|     The daemon is an **autonomous agent spawner** that operates in a **poll-spawn-complete cycle**: polls beads for `triage:ready` issues, infers skill from issue type, spawns within capacity limits, monitors for `Phase: Complete`, verifies and closes. The daemon operates **independently of orchestrators** - orchestrators triage (label issues ready), daemon spawns (batch processing), orchestrators synthesize (review completed work). Skill inference uses **issue type** (not labels) to map task→investigation, bug→systematic-debugging, etc. Capacity management uses **WorkerPool** with reconciliation against OpenCode to free stale slots.
00056|     
00057|     ---
00058|   - Why This Fails:
00059|     ### 1. Capacity Starvation
00060|     
00061|     **What happens:** Pool shows MaxAgents active, but `orch status` shows fewer actual agents running.
00062|     
00063|     **Root cause:** Spawn failures don't release slots. Agent spawned, counted against pool, but spawn fails (bad skill name, missing context, etc.) - slot never released.
00064|     
00065|     **Why detection is hard:** Pool only knows about attempts, not outcomes. No feedback loop from spawn failure to pool.
00066|     
00067|     **Fix:** Reconciliation with OpenCode. Query actual sessions, release slots for non-existent agents.
00068|     
00069|     **Prevention:** Spawn tracking with retry limits (`pkg/daemon/spawn_tracker.go`).
00070|     
00071|     ### 2. Duplicate Spawns
00072|     
00073|     **What happens:** Same issue spawned multiple times by daemon on consecutive polls.
00074|     
00075|     **Root cause:** Spawn latency. Issue labeled `triage:ready` at poll N, daemon spawns, but spawn hasn't transitioned issue to `in_progress` by poll N+1. Daemon sees same issue still ready, spawns again.
00076|     
00077|     **Why detection is hard:** Race condition between poll interval (60s) and spawn transition time (variable).
00078|     
00079|     **Fix:** Spawn deduplication via tracking. Track spawned beads IDs in memory, skip on subsequent polls until status confirms transition.
00080|     
00081|     **Source:** `pkg/daemon/spawn_tracker.go`
00082|     
00083|     ### 3. Skill Inference Mismatch
00084|     
00085|     **What happens:** Daemon spawns wrong skill for issue type.
00086|     
00087|     **Root cause:** Issue type doesn't match actual work needed. User creates `task` for implementation work (should be `feature`), daemon infers `investigation`.
00088|     
00089|     **Why detection is hard:** Type is set at creation, can't be changed by daemon. Daemon trusts type.
00090|     
00091|     **Fix:** Manual override. Orchestrator updates issue type before labeling `triage:ready`, or spawns manually with correct skill.
00092|     
00093|     **Prevention:** Better issue creation prompts, type validation, skill override via labels (future).

00105| - Beads SQLite Database Corruption
00106|   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/beads-database-corruption.md
00107|   - Summary:
00108|     Beads SQLite corruption occurs when the daemon enters a **rapid restart loop** (any failure → retry → fail → retry). Each cycle opens/closes the database performing WAL checkpoint. High-frequency checkpoints across unstable conditions (sandbox filesystem, legacy validation, any daemon failure) create opportunities for incomplete WAL operations, manifesting as **0-byte WAL files** that corrupt the database. The fix is **preventing rapid restarts**, not fixing individual failure causes.
00109|     
00110|     ---
00111|   - Why This Fails:
00112|     ### 1. No Backoff on Daemon Failure
00113|     
00114|     **What happens:** Daemon fails → restarts immediately → fails → restarts → cycles indefinitely.
00115|     
00116|     **Root cause:** No exponential backoff between restart attempts. launchd `KeepAlive` causes immediate restart.
00117|     
00118|     **Why detection is hard:** Each individual failure looks like "bad luck" - only aggregate pattern reveals problem.
00119|     
00120|     **Fix:** Implement minimum interval between daemon starts (e.g., 30 seconds).
00121|     
00122|     ### 2. Sandbox Environment Not Detected Early
00123|     
00124|     **What happens:** Daemon starts inside Claude Code sandbox, tries to chmod socket, fails, but has already opened database.
00125|     
00126|     **Root cause:** Sandbox detection happens AFTER database open, not before daemon start.
00127|     
00128|     **Fix:** Detect sandbox at CLI entry point, skip daemon auto-start entirely.
00129|     
00130|     ### 3. Legacy Database Validation Fails Late
00131|     
00132|     **What happens:** Database opens successfully, WAL enabled, THEN fingerprint validation fails.
00133|     
00134|     **Root cause:** Validation is post-open check, not pre-open gate.
00135|     
00136|     **Fix:** Check fingerprint before enabling WAL mode.
00137|     
00138|     ### 4. No Health Gate Before Operations
00139|     
00140|     **What happens:** Daemon starts despite known-bad state (missing fingerprint, sandbox environment).
00141|     
00142|     **Root cause:** No pre-flight checks before daemon entry point.
00143|     
00144|     **Fix:** `bd daemon start` should validate prerequisites before proceeding.
00145|     
00146|     ---
00147|   - Your findings should confirm, contradict, or extend the claims above.

The architecture creates a workspace with SPAWN_CONTEXT.md embedding skill content + task description + kb context, then launches an OpenCode session. The tier system (light/full) determines whether SYNTHESIS.md is required at completion. Triage friction (`--bypass-triage` flag) intentionally makes manual spawns harder to encourage daemon-driven workflow.

00155|     1. **Workspace name = kebab-case task description** - Used for tmux window, directory name, session title
00156|     2. **Beads ID required for phase reporting** - `--no-track` creates untracked IDs that can't report to beads
00157|     3. **KB context uses --global flag** - Cross-repo constraints are essential
00158|     4. **Skill content stripped for --no-track** - Beads instructions removed when not tracking
00159|     5. **Session scoping is per-project** - `orch send` only works within same directory hash

00195|     ### Failure Mode 3: Daemon Spawns Blocked Issues

00219| - Beads Integration Architecture
00220|   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/beads-integration-architecture.md
00221|   - Summary:
00222|     Beads integration uses **RPC-first with CLI fallback** pattern: try native Go RPC client (fast, no process spawn), fall back to CLI subprocess if daemon unavailable. The integration operates at **three points in agent lifecycle**: spawn (create issue), work (report phase via comments), complete (close with reason). **Auto-tracking** creates issues automatically unless `--no-track` flag set. The RPC client lives in **pkg/beads** (never shell out with `exec.Command` directly) and provides 10x performance improvement over CLI (single RPC call vs subprocess spawn + JSON parse).
00223|     
00224|     ---
00225|   - Why This Fails:
00226|     ### 1. RPC Client Unavailable
00227|     
00228|     **What happens:** RPC calls fail, client falls back to CLI, performance degrades.
00229|     
00230|     **Root cause:** Beads daemon not running. RPC socket `~/.beads/daemon.sock` doesn't exist.
00231|     
00232|     **Why detection is hard:** Fallback is silent. No warning that RPC failed. User sees slow performance, doesn't know why.
00233|     
00234|     **Fix:** Start beads daemon: `bd daemon start` or ensure launchd starts it on boot.
00235|     
00236|     **Detection:** Log RPC failures, surface in `orch doctor` health check.
00237|     
00238|     ### 2. Beads ID Not Found
00239|     
00240|     **What happens:** `orch complete orch-go-abc1` fails with "issue not found".
00241|     
00242|     **Root cause:** Cross-project spawn. Issue created in orch-knowledge, but trying to complete from orch-go. Beads scoped to current directory's `.beads/`.
00243|     
00244|     **Why detection is hard:** Beads ID looks valid (correct format), but doesn't exist in current project's `.beads/issues.jsonl`.
00245|     
00246|     **Fix:** `cd` into correct project before completion, or use `--workdir` flag.
00247|     
00248|     **Prevention:** `orch complete` should detect project from workspace, auto-cd.
00249|     
00250|     ### 3. Auto-Tracking Creates Duplicates
00251|     
00252|     **What happens:** `orch spawn` creates issue, but issue already exists for same work.
00253|     
00254|     **Root cause:** User creates issue manually, then spawns with auto-tracking. Both create issue.
00255|     
00256|     **Why detection is hard:** No deduplication. Beads doesn't check if similar issue exists.
00257|     
00258|     **Fix:** Use `--issue <id>` flag to reference existing issue instead of auto-creating.
00259|     
00260|     **Prevention:** Better UX: `orch spawn` could check for related issues and prompt user.

00271| - Orchestrator Session Lifecycle
00272|   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/orchestrator-session-lifecycle.md
00273|   - Summary:
00274|     Orchestrator sessions operate in a **three-tier hierarchy** (meta-orchestrator → orchestrator → worker) where each level is completed by the level above. Orchestrators produce **SESSION_HANDOFF.md** (not SYNTHESIS.md) and **wait** for completion (not /exit). They track via **session registry** (not beads) because orchestrators manage conversations, not work items. Frame collapse occurs when orchestrators drop levels and do work below their station - detected externally, not self-diagnosed. Checkpoint discipline uses duration thresholds (2h/3h/4h) as a proxy for context exhaustion.
00275|     
00276|     ---
00277|   - Why This Fails:
00278|     ### 1. Frame Collapse (Orchestrator → Worker)
00279|     
00280|     **What happens:** Orchestrator drops into worker-level implementation (editing code, debugging, investigating).
00281|     
00282|     **Root cause:** Vague goals → exploration mode → investigation → debugging. **Framing cues override skill instructions.**
00283|     
00284|     **Why detection is hard:** Orchestrators can't self-diagnose frame collapse. The frame defines what's visible, so from inside the collapsed frame, the behavior feels appropriate.
00285|     
00286|     **Detection signals:**
00287|     - Edit tool usage on code files (not orchestration artifacts)
00288|     - Time spent >15 minutes on direct fixes
00289|     - SESSION_HANDOFF.md shows "Manual fixes" sections
00290|     - Post-mortem reveals work that should have been spawned
00291|     
00292|     **NOT the fix:** Adding more ABSOLUTE DELEGATION RULE warnings. The agent already knows. The problem is framing, not awareness.
00293|     
00294|     **Prevention:**
00295|     1. Provide specific goals with action verbs, concrete deliverables, success criteria
00296|     2. Use WHICH vs HOW test: meta decides WHICH focus, orchestrator decides HOW to execute
00297|     3. Frame collapse check in SESSION_HANDOFF.md template
00298|     4. Potential: OpenCode plugin tracking Edit usage on code vs artifacts
00299|     
00300|     **Trigger pattern:** Failure-to-implementation. After agents fail, orchestrator tries to "just fix it" instead of trying different spawn strategy.
00301|     
00302|     ### 2. Self-Termination Attempts
00303|     
00304|     **What happens:** Spawned orchestrator tries to run `orch session end` or `/exit` instead of waiting for completion.
00305|     
00306|     **Root cause:** ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md template contradicted the hierarchical completion model (told orchestrator to self-terminate).
00307|     
00308|     **Why it's wrong:** Breaks the "completed by level above" invariant. Orchestrator can't verify its own work from meta perspective.
00309|     
00310|     **Fix:** Template updated Jan 2026 to instruct "write SESSION_HANDOFF.md and WAIT".
00311|     
00312|     ### 3. Session Registry Drift
00313|     
00314|     **What happens:** `~/.orch/sessions.json` shows status "active" for completed sessions.
00315|     
00316|     **Root cause:** `orch complete` didn't update session registry status (only closed beads issues or removed registry entries).
00317|     
00318|     **Why it matters:** Stale active sessions accumulate, `orch status` shows ghost sessions, registry becomes unreliable.
00319|     
00320|     **Fix:** `orch complete` now updates status to "completed", `orch abandon` updates to "abandoned" - sessions preserved for history, not removed.

00339| - macOS Click Freeze
00340|   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/macos-click-freeze.md
00341|   - Summary:
00342|     Trackpad clicks stop registering every ~15 minutes while cursor movement and keyboard continue working. `sudo killall -HUP WindowServer` fixes it every time (HUP = reconfigure, not restart). This points to WindowServer accumulating corrupted state in its click event pipeline. **Breakthrough in Session 15:** nuclear elimination of ~23 services stopped the freeze. Karabiner reinstalled (upgraded 14.13→15.9.0) — **no freeze with Karabiner running**, further confirming H2 elimination. NI HardwareAgent and Ollama fully uninstalled (not just disabled). System stable under full agent workload (3 concurrent spawns + Karabiner + OpenCode + orch). **Still narrowing:** ~18 user LaunchAgents + 3 system LaunchDaemons remain disabled. Next: re-enable in phases to isolate culprit.
00343|     
00344|     ---
00345|   - Why This Fails:
00346|     ### Hypothesis 1: yabai event interception corrupts WindowServer state — ELIMINATED
00347|     
00348|     yabai uses the macOS Accessibility API to manage windows. Freeze recurred with yabai fully stopped (`yabai --stop-service`, confirmed no process via `pgrep`).
00349|     
00350|     **Evidence against:** Freeze recurred within ~30 minutes with yabai completely stopped. No yabai process running. Definitively eliminated.
00351|     
00352|     ### Hypothesis 2: Karabiner DriverKit drops click events at kernel level — ELIMINATED
00353|     
00354|     Karabiner was fully uninstalled (app removed, DriverKit extension gone, no IOKit registry entries, no LaunchAgents). Freeze persisted immediately after fresh reboot with no Karabiner components present.
00355|     
00356|     **Evidence against:** Completely uninstalled — no process, no DriverKit extension (`systemextensionsctl list` clean), no IOKit entries (`ioreg` clean), no LaunchAgents. Freeze still occurred immediately post-reboot. Definitively eliminated.
00357|     
00358|     ### Hypothesis 3: WindowServer internal corruption (no external cause) — WEAKENED
00359|     
00360|     macOS 15.6.1 (Sequoia) may have a bug where WindowServer's click event routing table gets corrupted over time. This would be independent of any third-party software.
00361|     
00362|     **Evidence for:** Would explain why eliminating multiple apps in Session 11 didn't fix it.
00363|     
00364|     **Evidence against:** Three research probes (2026-02-11) searched GitHub, Reddit, Apple Discussions exhaustively — **zero matching reports** for this symptom pattern (clicks stop, cursor moves, HUP fixes). If this were a Sequoia bug, community reports would exist. This significantly weakens H3.
00365|     
00366|     ### Hypothesis 4: Memory pressure from OpenCode instance accumulation — WEAKENED
00367|     
00368|     OpenCode accumulates instances (with LSP/MCP/file watchers) per unique project directory. Each instance costs 300-500MB for LSP alone.
00369|     
00370|     **Evidence against (Session 15):**
00371|     - Freeze occurred **immediately after fresh reboot** — memory was abundant, OpenCode hadn't even started yet
00372|     - This strongly suggests memory pressure is NOT the primary cause

00377| 	if d.Config.Label == "" {
00378| 		return issues, epicChildIDs
00379| 	}
00380| 
00381| 	// Find epics with the required label
00382| 	var epicsToExpand []string
00383| 	existingIDs := make(map[string]bool)
00384| 	for _, issue := range issues {
00385| 		existingIDs[issue.ID] = true
00386| 		if issue.IssueType == "epic" && issue.HasLabel(d.Config.Label) {
00387| 			epicsToExpand = append(epicsToExpand, issue.ID)
00388| 			if d.Config.Verbose {
00389| 				fmt.Printf("  DEBUG: Found triage:ready epic %s, will include children\n", issue.ID)
00390| 			}
00391| 		}
00392| 	}
00393| 
00394| 	// No epics to expand
00395| 	if len(epicsToExpand) == 0 {
00396| 		return issues, epicChildIDs
00397| 	}
00398| 
00399| 	// Expand each epic by fetching its children
00400| 	listChildren := d.listEpicChildrenFunc
00401| 	if listChildren == nil {
00402| 		listChildren = ListEpicChildren
00403| 	}
00404| 	for _, epicID := range epicsToExpand {
00405| 		children, err := listChildren(epicID)
00406| 		if err != nil {
00407| 			if d.Config.Verbose {
00408| 				fmt.Printf("  DEBUG: Warning: could not list children of epic %s: %v\n", epicID, err)
00409| 			}
00410| 			continue
00411| 		}
00412| 
00413| 		for _, child := range children {
00414| 			// Skip closed children - they shouldn't be spawned
00415| 			if child.Status == "closed" {
00416| 				if d.Config.Verbose {
00417| 					fmt.Printf("  DEBUG: Skipping closed epic child %s (from parent %s)\n", child.ID, epicID)
00418| 				}
00419| 				continue
00420| 			}
00421| 			// Only add if not already in the list
00422| 			if !existingIDs[child.ID] {
00423| 				issues = append(issues, child)
00424| 				existingIDs[child.ID] = true
00425| 				epicChildIDs[child.ID] = true
00426| 				if d.Config.Verbose {
00427| 					fmt.Printf("  DEBUG: Added epic child %s (from parent %s)\n", child.ID, epicID)
00428| 				}
00429| 			} else {
00430| 				// Already in list, but mark as epic child for label exemption
00431| 				epicChildIDs[child.ID] = true
00432| 			}
00433| 		}
00434| 	}
00435| 
00436| 	return issues, epicChildIDs
00437| }

00542| // Preview shows what would be processed next without actually processing.
00543| // It also collects all rejected issues with their rejection reasons.
00544| func (d *Daemon) Preview() (*PreviewResult, error) {
00545| 	result := &PreviewResult{}
00546| 
00547| 	// Check rate limit status
00548| 	if d.RateLimiter != nil {
00549| 		canSpawn, count, msg := d.RateLimiter.CanSpawn()
00550| 		result.RateLimited = !canSpawn
00551| 		if d.RateLimiter.MaxPerHour > 0 {
00552| 			result.RateStatus = fmt.Sprintf("%d/%d spawns in last hour", count, d.RateLimiter.MaxPerHour)
00553| 		}
00554| 		if !canSpawn {
00555| 			result.Message = msg
00556| 			// Still collect rejected issues even if rate limited
00557| 		}
00558| 	}
00559| 
00560| 	// Get all issues and categorize them
00561| 	issues, err := d.listIssuesFunc()
00562| 	if err != nil {
00563| 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to list issues: %w", err)
00564| 	}
00565| 
00566| 	// Expand triage:ready epics by including their children
00567| 	issues, epicChildIDs := d.expandTriageReadyEpics(issues)
00568| 
00569| 	// Sort by priority (lower number = higher priority)
00570| 	sort.Slice(issues, func(i, j int) bool {
00571| 		return issues[i].Priority < issues[j].Priority
00572| 	})
00573| 
00574| 	var spawnable *Issue
00575| 	for _, issue := range issues {
00576| 		// Check each rejection reason in order and collect all rejected issues
00577| 		reason := d.checkRejectionReasonWithEpicChildren(issue, epicChildIDs)
00578| 		if reason != "" {
00579| 			result.RejectedIssues = append(result.RejectedIssues, RejectedIssue{
00580| 				Issue:  issue,
00581| 				Reason: reason,
00582| 			})
00583| 			continue
00584| 		}
00585| 
00586| 		// Found a spawnable issue - take the first one (highest priority)
00587| 		if spawnable == nil {
00588| 			issueCopy := issue
00589| 			spawnable = &issueCopy
00590| 		}
00591| 	}
00592| 
00593| 	// If rate limited, we still collected rejected issues but can't spawn
00594| 	if result.RateLimited {
00595| 		return result, nil
00596| 	}
00597| 
00598| 	if spawnable == nil {
00599| 		result.Message = "No spawnable issues in queue"
00600| 		return result, nil
00601| 	}
00602| 
00603| 	skill, err := InferSkillFromIssue(spawnable)
00604| 	if err != nil {
00605| 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to infer skill: %w", err)
00606| 	}
00607| 
00608| 	result.Issue = spawnable
00609| 	result.Skill = skill
00610| 
00611| 	// Check for hotspot warnings if checker is configured
00612| 	if d.HotspotChecker != nil {
00613| 		result.HotspotWarnings = CheckHotspotsForIssue(spawnable, d.HotspotChecker)
00614| 	}
00615| 
00616| 	return result, nil
00617| }

00619| // checkRejectionReason checks if an issue should be rejected and returns the reason.

00626| // checkRejectionReasonWithEpicChildren checks if an issue should be rejected and returns the reason.
00627| // The epicChildIDs map contains IDs of issues that are children of triage:ready epics.
00628| // These children are exempt from the label requirement check.
00629| // Returns empty string if the issue is spawnable.
00630| func (d *Daemon) checkRejectionReasonWithEpicChildren(issue Issue, epicChildIDs map[string]bool) string {
00631| 	// Check for empty/missing type first (the main problem case from the bug report)
00632| 	if issue.IssueType == "" {
00633| 		return "missing type (required for skill inference)"
00634| 	}
00635| 
00636| 	// Check for non-spawnable type
00637| 	// Note: Epics with triage:ready are not spawnable themselves, but their children are.
00638| 	// The message is informative to explain why epics are rejected.
00639| 	if !IsSpawnableType(issue.IssueType) {
00640| 		if issue.IssueType == "epic" && issue.HasLabel(d.Config.Label) {
00641| 			return fmt.Sprintf("type 'epic' not spawnable (children will be processed instead)")
00642| 		}
00643| 		return fmt.Sprintf("type '%s' not spawnable (must be bug/feature/task/investigation)", issue.IssueType)
00644| 	}
00645| 
00646| 	// Check for blocked status
00647| 	if issue.Status == "blocked" {
00648| 		return "status is blocked"
00649| 	}
00650| 
00651| 	// Check for in_progress status
00652| 	if issue.Status == "in_progress" {
00653| 		return "status is in_progress (already being worked on)"
00654| 	}
00655| 
00656| 	// Check for missing required label
00657| 	// Epic children are exempt from this check - they inherit triage status from parent
00658| 	if d.Config.Label != "" && !issue.HasLabel(d.Config.Label) {
00659| 		if epicChildIDs == nil || !epicChildIDs[issue.ID] {
00660| 			return fmt.Sprintf("missing label '%s'", d.Config.Label)
00661| 		}
00662| 		// Epic child - exempt from label requirement
00663| 	}
00664| 
00665| 	// Check for blocking dependencies
00666| 	blockers, err := beads.CheckBlockingDependencies(issue.ID)
00667| 	if err == nil && len(blockers) > 0 {
00668| 		var blockerIDs []string
00669| 		for _, b := range blockers {
00670| 			blockerIDs = append(blockerIDs, fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", b.ID, b.Status))
00671| 		}
00672| 		return fmt.Sprintf("blocked by dependencies: %s", strings.Join(blockerIDs, ", "))
00673| 	}
00674| 
00675| 	return "" // Spawnable
00676| }

00717| // Once processes a single issue from the queue and returns.

00725| // OnceExcluding processes a single issue from the queue, excluding skipped issues.
00726| // This allows the daemon to skip issues that failed to spawn (e.g., due to failure
00727| // report gate) and continue processing other issues in the queue.
00728| //
00729| // The skip map should contain issue IDs that should be skipped this cycle.
00730| // If a worker pool is configured, it acquires a slot before spawning.
00731| // If a rate limiter is configured, it checks the hourly limit before spawning.
00732| func (d *Daemon) OnceExcluding(skip map[string]bool) (*OnceResult, error) {
00733| 	// Check rate limit first (before fetching issues)
00734| 	if d.RateLimiter != nil {
00735| 		canSpawn, count, msg := d.RateLimiter.CanSpawn()
00736| 		if !canSpawn {
00737| 			if d.Config.Verbose {
00738| 				fmt.Printf("  Rate limited: %s\n", msg)
00739| 			}
00740| 			return &OnceResult{
00741| 				Processed: false,
00742| 				Message:   fmt.Sprintf("Rate limited: %d/%d spawns in the last hour", count, d.RateLimiter.MaxPerHour),
00743| 			}, nil
00744| 		}
00745| 	}
00746| 
00747| 	issue, err := d.NextIssueExcluding(skip)
00748| 	if err != nil {
00749| 		return nil, err
00750| 	}
00751| 
00752| 	if issue == nil {
00753| 		return &OnceResult{
00754| 			Processed: false,
00755| 			Message:   "No spawnable issues in queue",
00756| 		}, nil
00757| 	}
00758| 
00759| 	skill, err := InferSkillFromIssue(issue)
00760| 	if err != nil {
00761| 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to infer skill: %w", err)
00762| 	}
00763| 
00764| 	// Session-level dedup: Check if there's an existing OpenCode session for this issue.
00765| 	// This prevents duplicate spawns when:
00766| 	// 1. SpawnedIssueTracker TTL expires (5min/6h) but agent is still running
00767| 	// 2. Status update to "in_progress" failed silently
00768| 	// 3. Multiple daemon instances try to spawn the same issue
00769| 	if HasExistingSessionForBeadsID(issue.ID) {
00770| 		if d.Config.Verbose {
00771| 			fmt.Printf("  DEBUG: Skipping %s (existing OpenCode session found)\n", issue.ID)
00772| 		}
00773| 		return &OnceResult{
00774| 			Processed: false,
00775| 			Issue:     issue,
00776| 			Skill:     skill,
00777| 			Message:   fmt.Sprintf("Existing session found for %s - skipping to prevent duplicate", issue.ID),
00778| 		}, nil
00779| 	}
00780| 
00781| 	// If pool is configured, acquire a slot first
00782| 	var slot *Slot
00783| 	if d.Pool != nil {
00784| 		slot = d.Pool.TryAcquire()
00785| 		if slot == nil {
00786| 			return &OnceResult{
00787| 				Processed: false,
00788| 				Issue:     issue,
00789| 				Skill:     skill,
00790| 				Message:   "At capacity - no slots available",
00791| 			}, nil
00792| 		}
00793| 		slot.BeadsID = issue.ID
00794| 	}
00795| 
00796| 	// Mark issue as spawned BEFORE calling spawnFunc to prevent race condition.
00797| 	// This prevents duplicate spawns if daemon polls again before beads status updates.
00798| 	if d.SpawnedIssues != nil {
00799| 		d.SpawnedIssues.MarkSpawned(issue.ID)
00800| 	}
00801| 
00802| 	// Spawn the work
00803| 	if err := d.spawnFunc(issue.ID); err != nil {
00804| 		// Unmark on spawn failure so issue can be retried
00805| 		if d.SpawnedIssues != nil {
00806| 			d.SpawnedIssues.Unmark(issue.ID)
00807| 		}
00808| 		// Release slot on spawn failure
00809| 		if d.Pool != nil && slot != nil {
00810| 			d.Pool.Release(slot)
00811| 		}
00812| 		return &OnceResult{
00813| 			Processed: false,
00814| 			Issue:     issue,
00815| 			Skill:     skill,
00816| 			Error:     err,
00817| 			Message:   fmt.Sprintf("Failed to spawn: %v", err),
00818| 		}, nil
00819| 	}
00820| 
00821| 	// Record successful spawn for rate limiting
00822| 	if d.RateLimiter != nil {
00823| 		d.RateLimiter.RecordSpawn()
00824| 	}
00825| 
00826| 	return &OnceResult{
00827| 		Processed: true,
00828| 		Issue:     issue,
00829| 		Skill:     skill,
00830| 		Message:   fmt.Sprintf("Spawned work on %s", issue.ID),
00831| 	}, nil
00832| }
